title: Panella supersede confusion-matrix goldset
description: A synthetic goldset for scoring a fact-supersede/confusion-matrix classifier: given pairs of facts, does the classifier correctly label each pair supersede/coexist/unrelated, and does it correctly identify the currently-true fact set? See eval/goldsets/SCHEMA.md for label definitions and worked examples. Deterministic ordering: cases sorted by case_id; pairs within a case sorted by (earlier_id, later_id). v1 adds an optional per-pair `high_risk` flag (see `$defs/pair`) for high-risk/sensitive slots (medication, legal name, emergency contact, home-address-sharing, primary physician, dietary restriction). v1.1 adds a REQUIRED per-fact `probe` object (see `$defs/probe`): a simulated extractor emission for the fact — DATA for a downstream resolver to reconcile, never gold truth (the pair label stays the gold).

seed.description: The fixed PRNG seed synth_supersede.py used to generate this file (determinism proof).

case_id.description: Stable, deterministic identifier for this case (e.g. 'sc-employer-0007').

facts.description: Ordered list of facts in this case, oldest-observed first.

pairs.description: Every judged pair among this case's facts. A case with N facts need not judge all C(N,2) pairs — only the pairs that matter for the scenario — but every judged pair MUST reference fact_ids present in `facts`.

current_truth.description: The fact_ids an IDEAL system reports as currently true for this case (i.e. not superseded by any later fact), each with a one-line rationale.

fact_id.description: Stable identifier for this fact, unique within the case.

content.description: Synthetic fact content — NEVER real names/data (see SCHEMA.md).

date.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp this fact was observed/stated.

probe.description: Required (v1.1+). A SIMULATED EXTRACTOR EMISSION for this fact — what a plausible extractor would output before any resolution. Probes are DATA a downstream resolver reconciles against the gold pair labels, never truth themselves: a supersede pair's two probes deliberately use DIFFERENT raw_domain surfaces from the slot's pool (key-stability pressure), and hr trap facts carry a DELIBERATELY mis-domained benign-looking raw_domain (the hr-escalation trap). See SCHEMA.md's probe section and synth_supersede.py's SLOT_SURFACES / HR_MISDOMAIN_SURFACES.

kind.description: The emitted kind — the slot's kind in the preference|fact|constraint taxonomy.

raw_domain.description: The emitted surface domain: a normalize_domain-stable snake_case string, drawn from the slot's surface pool — or, for hr trap facts, from the slot's deliberate mis-domain pool.

value.description: The short extracted attribute value consistent with the fact content (not the whole sentence).

earlier_id.description: fact_id of the chronologically earlier fact in the pair (date <= later_id's date).

later_id.description: fact_id of the chronologically later fact in the pair.

label.description: supersede = later_id replaces earlier_id as the current truth for the same slot; coexist = both remain true simultaneously in the SAME aspect (life domain) but DIFFERENT slots (same-cluster facts, neither updating the other); unrelated = the two facts live in different aspects and share no slot/subject (a classifier merging them would be a false collision). coexist-vs-unrelated is a mechanical biconditional over synth_supersede.py's aspect tags — see eval/goldsets/SCHEMA.md.

high_risk.description: Optional (v1+). True when at least one fact in this pair concerns a high-risk/sensitive slot (e.g. medication, legal name, emergency contact, home-address-sharing, primary physician, dietary restriction). Absent/false for ordinary benign pairs. Used by score_supersede.py's hr-slice metrics (hr_supersede_recall, hr_false_merge_count, hr_coverage) to isolate the highest-consequence classification failures — a false merge on a high_risk unrelated pair is more dangerous than an ordinary one.

rationale.description: One line explaining why this fact is currently true (e.g. 'latest employer-update fact, not superseded by any later pair').
